text.title: kb-symbol-index-fresh-tags Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for kb-symbol-index-fresh-tags. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The repo MUST commit a symbol index (e.g. tags, .serena/symbols.db, or LSIF/SCIP file) at a known path declared in the project AGENTS.md. A pre-commit hook MUST regenerate the index from staged changes and re-stage it. CI MUST run scripts/check_tags_fresh.sh (or equivalent) — regenerate into a temp dir, diff against the committed copy, exit non-zero on any difference. Vendored, generated, and build-output dirs MUST be in the index exclude list.
rule.rationale: Agents that consult an out-of-date index produce confidently wrong answers (point to deleted files, miss new functions). The drift gate is the only mechanic that keeps the artifact trustworthy across long-lived repos and parallel branches. The exclude list keeps the index from being dominated by node_modules-class noise.

rule.statement: The project AGENTS.md MUST declare the symbol index path and required lookup order: agents query the index for any "where is <Symbol> defined", "find references to <Symbol>", or "what calls <Symbol>" question; agents fall back to grep ONLY for non-symbol literals (strings, regex patterns, comments). Coding-agent system prompts (Claude Code skill, Cursor rule, Codex AGENTS.md) MUST embed this protocol verbatim. PR templates MUST include a checkbox: "Symbol-index queries used where applicable".
rule.rationale: Forcing the index to be the first lookup cuts agent context cost on real refactors by 5-20x (Cognition Devin and Cursor team published numbers in 2025-2026). The grep fallback for non-symbol literals stops the rule from being absolute and unworkable. Embedding the protocol in AGENTS.md and the PR template gates both runtime behaviour and review behaviour with the same source of truth.
